title: acer saccharum f. monumentale
intro: Acer saccharum f. monumentale, also known as the Bigleaf Maple or Mountain Sugar Maple, is a large deciduous tree species that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is valued for its sap, which can be collected and boiled down to produce a sweet syrup, as well as its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Acer saccharum f. monumentale are numerous, and include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high concentration of salicylic acid and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of Acer saccharum f. monumentale include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the bark, leaves, or sap in water or other solvents.
